content.problemStatement:
There are NNN people standing in a queue for Chef's ice cream. They are numbered 111 through NNN from the front to the back of the queue.

Chef's customers do not like to wait too long. For each valid iii, we know that the ice cream ordered by the iii-th person will take PiP_iPi‚Äã seconds to prepare and cost ViV_iVi‚Äã units of money, but if this person has to wait strictly more than WiW_iWi‚Äã seconds in the queue (until Chef starts preparing their order), they will leave. At any time, it is only allowed to prepare the ice cream of the person standing at the front of the queue.

Chef wants to maximise his profit (the sum of prices of sold ice creams). In order to do that, he may choose to kick some of the customers (possibly nobody) out of the queue. Find the maximum profit Chef can make.
constraints: 1‚â§Pi,Wi,Vi‚â§1091 \le P_i, W_i, V_i \le 10^91‚â§Pi‚Äã,Wi‚Äã,Vi‚Äã‚â§109 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), each of these lines contains three space-separated integers WiW_iWi‚Äã, PiP_iPi‚Äã and ViV_iVi‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum possible profit.
